name: Pumpkin Nut Cake in a Jar
recipeIngredient: 1 jar wide mouth with lids and rings (8 pint)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the shortening and sugar together, adding sugar slowly. Beat in the eggs, pumpkin, and water. Set aside.
2. Sift together the flour, baking powder, salt, ground cloves, allspice, cinnamon, and baking soda. Add to the pumpkin mixture and stir well. Stir in the nuts.
3. Pour batter into greased canning jars, filling half-full. Place jars on a cookie sheet. Bake upright in a preheated 325 degree oven for about 45 minutes. (The cake will rise and pull away from the sides of the jar.)
4. When done, remove one jar at a time from the oven while still warm. Place a wax paper circle on the top end of the cake. Wipe the sealing edge of the jar. Place the lid on the jar and close tightly with the ring.
5. Turn the jar upside down (the cake will loosen at this time). Repeat with other jars. Leave jars upside down until sealed. (The jar is sealed if the lid remains flat when pressed in the center.)
6. To serve: Open the jar, slide a knife around inside of the jar to loosen the cake, and remove the cake from the jar. Warm the cake in the oven if desired. Slice and serve with whipped cream.
This dessert treat is baked and presented in a wide-mouth canning jar. Because the jar is sealed, the cake keeps indefinitely. It can be made ahead of the holidays.
